---
title: ""
page-layout: full
---

<<<<<<< HEAD
::: {.home-shell}
::: {.hero-grid}
::: {.hero-copy}
::: {.section-label}
Path-signature coordinates for spectral-line morphology
:::

# spectropath {.hero-name}

Spectropath computes a compact set of low-order path-signature coordinates for
spectral lines written as ordered paths \(X=(u,F)\).

The package follows the notation used in the paper:
`p2`, `p3u`, `p3F`, `p4F`, `p4T`, and `p_pm`.
It is meant for the cases where line shape matters:
blue and red wings, shoulders, broad bases, double peaks, and mixed
emission--absorption structure.
=======
:::: {.home-shell}

:::: {.hero-grid}

:::: {.hero-copy}

<span class="section-label">Path-signature diagnostics for spectral-line morphology</span>

# spectropath {.hero-name}

Spectropath is a small R package for compact geometric descriptors of ordered spectral-line profiles.

It maps an ordered coordinate and a measured line profile into low-order coordinates for asymmetry, core-versus-wing structure, higher-order modulation, and emission--absorption ordering.

Use it when line shape matters: blue and red wings, shoulders, broad bases, double peaks, and mixed emission--absorption structure.
>>>>>>> b5ecb232ae03aa2b4e66dc5a4fae63446363b6d6

::: {.inline-links}
[Quick start](#quick-start)
[Examples](examples.html)
<<<<<<< HEAD
[Python](python.html)
=======
>>>>>>> b5ecb232ae03aa2b4e66dc5a4fae63446363b6d6
[Reference](reference.html)
[GitHub](https://github.com/RafaelSdeSouza/spectropath)
:::

<<<<<<< HEAD
::: {.code-card}
Minimal workflow
=======
::::

:::: {.code-card}

Example
>>>>>>> b5ecb232ae03aa2b4e66dc5a4fae63446363b6d6

```r
library(spectropath)

u <- seq(-5, 5, length.out = 500)
f <- dnorm(u, 0, 1) + 0.25 * dnorm(u, 1.8, 0.45)
path <- cbind(u, f)

path_features(path)
```

```text
      p2   p_pm    p3u    p3F    p4F    p4T
 -0.3133 0.000 -0.0169 -0.1736 0.4534 0.0132
```

::::

::::

## Selected results {.section-heading}

<<<<<<< HEAD
::: {.showcase-grid}
::: {.showcase-lead}
![Controlled profile comparison strip.](site-assets/moffat_stress_cases_panel_boundary_regions_row1.png)

::: {.showcase-caption}
One of the controlled comparison strips from the paper, showing a case where a
path coordinate remains discriminative in a region where width-based summaries
overlap.
:::
:::

::: {.showcase-side}
<div class="kinematics-pair">
  <img src="site-assets/kinematics_context_crop.png" class="kinematics-slice context" alt="MaNGA galaxy context image">
  <img src="site-assets/kinematics_starlet_crop.png" class="kinematics-slice masked" alt="Starlet-masked kinematic map">
</div>

::: {.showcase-caption}
The single-object IFU example shown as galaxy context plus the starlet-masked
kinematic result used in the paper workflow.
=======
::: {.figure-grid}

::: {.image-card}
![](site-assets/toy_case_row1.png)

::: {.card-caption}
Toy line profiles used to compare classical and path-based descriptors.
:::
:::

::: {.image-card}
![](site-assets/signed_emabs_cases_panel_boundary_regions_row1.png)

::: {.card-caption}
Mixed-sign profiles where emission--absorption ordering becomes relevant.
:::
:::

::: {.image-card}
![](site-assets/kinematics_col1_maps.png)

::: {.card-caption}
Path-selected regions in an IFU cube, summarized as a compact kinematic map.
>>>>>>> b5ecb232ae03aa2b4e66dc5a4fae63446363b6d6
:::
:::

:::

## Paper notation {.section-heading}

::: {.notation-grid}

::: {.notation-item}
`p2`

Signed velocity--flux area.
:::

::: {.notation-item}
`p3u`

Location of asymmetry along the ordered coordinate.
:::

::: {.notation-item}
`p3F`

Whether asymmetry is concentrated in bright or faint parts of the line.
:::

::: {.notation-item}
`p4F`

Higher-order flux modulation.
:::

::: {.notation-item}
`p4T`

Twist-like higher-order structure.
:::

::: {.notation-item}
`p_pm`

Emission--absorption ordering.
:::

:::

## Quick start {#quick-start .section-heading}

::: {.quick-grid}

::: {.quick-card}

Install

```r
install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("RafaelSdeSouza/spectropath")
library(spectropath)
```

:::

::: {.quick-card}

Compute

```r
u <- seq(-5, 5, length.out = 500)
f <- dnorm(u, 0, 1) + 0.25 * dnorm(u, 1.8, 0.45)
path <- cbind(u, f)

path_features(path)
classical_features(path)
```

:::

:::

For a larger set of paper figures and small reproducible examples, see the [Examples](examples.html) page.

::::
